name: ICD Coding — Bariatric Complications + CLL + Multiple Comorbidities
description: Level 4 — MIMIC hadm 29757856 — 10+ diagnoses with bariatric surgery complications, oncology, metabolic
prompt: Patient: Male, 60 years old. Emergency admission. History of prior Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (bariatric surgery). Presenting with gastrojejunal ulcer, complications of bariatric procedure, and gastric fistula. Also: acute kidney failure (unspecified), chronic diastolic heart failure, delirium due to known physiological condition. Oncologic: chronic lymphocytic leukemia of B-cell type, not in remission. Metabolic/comorbidities: morbid obesity (BMI 40-44.9), type 2 diabetes mellitus with chronic kidney disease + polyneuropathy + hyperglycemia, hypothyroidism, hypertensive CKD (stage 3), pure hyperglyceridemia, paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, prior stroke with sequelae (left lower limb monoplegia), old MI, gout, bilateral hip osteoarthritis, shoulder osteoarthritis, obstructive sleep apnea, major depressive disorder (in remission), anxiety disorder. Status: long-term anticoagulant use, insulin use, cardiac pacemaker. Assign the appropriate ICD-10-CM code(s) with proper sequencing.

codes.description: Gastrojejunal ulcer, unspecified as acute or chronic, without hemorrhage or perforation
Other complications of other bariatric procedure
Delirium due to known physiological condition
Chronic lymphocytic leukemia of B-cell type not having achieved remission
Morbid (severe) obesity due to excess calories
Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease
Hypertensive chronic kidney disease with stage 1-4 CKD or unspecified CKD
